name: Ngrok
categoryDescription: Commonly abused tools that leave NO Windows registry artifacts. These tools are typically single executables, portable builds, or in-memory frameworks. Detection depends entirely on non-registry evidence: process execution logs, prefetch, ShimCache/Amcache, BAM/DAM, network traffic, and memory forensics. Includes detection notes for each tool.
description: Reverse proxy / tunneling tool that exposes local services over the internet via ngrok.com relay. Used by attackers to expose RDP, SMB, or C2 listeners without opening firewall ports. Single binary, no installer, no registry.
forensic_value: No registry artifacts. Network-level detection is primary — block/alert on ngrok.io DNS and IP ranges. CISA advisory AA22-320A references ngrok abuse.
